title: gymnanthemum extensum
intro: Gymnanthemum extensum, also known as the Mexican mint or shrubby hyssop, is a plant species in the Lamiaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory agent. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which are believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. Gymnanthemum extensum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and support overall well-being.
